description: In Tasmania, learner and provisional drivers must maintain zero BAC while driving.
In wet weather, reduce speed and increase following distance because roads can become slippery and visibility may decrease.
Drivers must slow down and give way to emergency vehicles displaying flashing lights or sirens.
Do not drive if alcohol, drugs, fatigue, illness or medication could affect your ability to drive safely.
Looking ahead, checking mirrors and scanning for hazards helps drivers react safely to changing road conditions.
Allow extra room around heavy vehicles and avoid driving in their blind spots.
Vehicles and trailers must stay within legal load and towing limits, and loads must be properly secured.
A NO U-TURN sign means drivers are not permitted to make a U-turn at that location.
Parking is prohibited too close to intersections, pedestrian crossings and traffic lights unless signs allow it.
Drivers must give way to pedestrians at crossings and when turning into a road pedestrians are crossing.
Tasmania Police may conduct random breath testing and drivers must provide a breath sample when requested.
School zones in Tasmania generally operate at 40 km/h during the times shown on school zone signs.
At a STOP sign or stop line, you must stop completely and give way before entering the intersection.
Leave a larger gap between vehicles in wet conditions to allow extra braking distance.
Avoid following trucks too closely because they reduce visibility and need more stopping distance.
Obey reduced speed limits, temporary signs and traffic controllers when driving through roadwork areas.
Bus lanes may only be used by authorised vehicles unless signs indicate permitted access.
Loading zones are reserved for authorised loading and unloading activities and restrictions apply to other vehicles.
